source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A comet in an orbit about the sun has a velocity $10\,\mathrm{km}/\mathrm{sec}$ at aphelion and $80\,\mathrm{km}/\mathrm{sec}$ at perihelion (Fig. 1.31). If the earth's velocity in a circular orbit is $30\,\mathrm{km}/\mathrm{sec}$ and the radius of its orbit is $1.5 \times 10^{8}\,\mathrm{km}$, find the aphelion distance $R_{\mathrm{a}}$ for the comet. (Wisconsin)

candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: A comet in an orbit about the sun has a velocity $10\,\mathrm{km}/\mathrm{sec}$ at aphelion and $80\,\mathrm{km}/\mathrm{sec}$ at perihelion (Fig. 1.31). If the earth's velocity in a circular orbit is $30\,\mathrm{km}/\mathrm{sec}$ and the radius of its orbit is $1.5 \times 10^{8}\,\mathrm{km}$, find the aphelion distance $R_{\mathrm{a}}$ for the comet. (Wisconsin)
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

extraction_notes: question references Fig. 1.31, but the record indicates vision relevance is optional

metadata.reasoning_chain:
Solution:
Let $v$ be the velocity of the earth, $R$ the radius of the earth's orbit, $m$ and $m_{s}$ the masses of the earth and the sun respectively. Then
$$ rac{m v^{2}}{R}= rac{G m m_{s}}{R^{2}}$$
or
$$G m_{s}=R v^{2}$$

By the conservation of the mechanical energy and of the angular momentum of the comet, we have
$$ egin{aligned}
 rac{-G m_{c} m_{s}}{R_{a}}+ rac{m_{c} v_{a}^{2}}{2} &= rac{-G m_{c} m_{s}}{R_{p}}+ rac{m_{c} v_{p}^{2}}{2}\
m_{c} R_{a} v_{a} &=m_{c} R_{p} v_{p}
\end{aligned}$$
where $m_{c}$ is the mass of the comet, and $v_{a}$ and $v_{p}$ are the velocities of the comet at aphelion and at perihelion respectively. The above equations give
$$R_{a}= rac{2 G m_{s}}{v_{a}(v_{a}+v_{p})}= rac{2 R v^{2}}{v_{a}(v_{a}+v_{p})}=3 	imes 10^{8}\ \mathrm{km}$$
